It compares single-agent tarlatamab (Imdelltra) against standard-of-care chemotherapy (country-dependent options like topotecan, lurbinectedin, or amrubicin) with patients randomized 1:1. The patient population includes individuals with relapsed/recurrent extensive-stage SCLC after prior platinum-based first-line chemotherapy (second-line setting). Key stratification factors are prior anti-PD-1/PD-L1 exposure, chemotherapy-free interval, presence of brain metastases, and the specific SOC agent used. The primary endpoint is overall survival (OS), with key secondary endpoints including progression-free survival (PFS), objective response rate (ORR), duration of response, and safety.
regulatory_designations: Breakthrough Therapy, Orphan Drug, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval (May 16, 2024), Full Approval (Nov 2025)
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Lurbinectedin, Topotecan, Sacituzumab govitecan, I‑DXd/DS‑17300, Tarlatamab
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
key_competing_drugs_trials: Lurbinectedin, Topotecan, Sacituzumab govitecan, I‑DXd/DS‑17300, other DLL3‑targeting agents under development
current_standard_of_care: First‑line extensive‑stage SCLC is treated with platinum/etoposide plus a PD‑L1 inhibitor (e.g., atezolizumab or durvalumab). Second‑line options include lurbinectedin, topotecan, and platinum rechallenge in platinum‑sensitive relapse; no approved therapies exist beyond third line.
primary_endpoints: Overall survival
target_population: Adults ≥18 with histologically/cytologically confirmed extensive‑stage SCLC after progression/relapse on one platinum‑based regimen; measurable disease per RECIST 1.1; ECOG 0‑1; adequate organ function; minimum life expectancy 12 weeks; asymptomatic treated/stable brain metastases allowed; symptomatic CNS metastases and leptomeningeal disease excluded.
trial_progress_evidence: Trial status noted as closed to accrual; randomized design with defined SOC comparator confirms advancement.
previous_phase_results_summary: Phase 1: 23% ORR with durable responses in heavily pre‑treated SCLC. Phase 2 DeLLphi‑301 (10 mg): ORR ~40%, median PFS 4.9 months, median OS 14.3 months.
secondary_endpoints: Secondary endpoints unknown
comparator_control_arm: Standard of care chemotherapy (lurbinectedin, topotecan, amrubicin)
study_design: Randomized, open-label, parallel assignment, Phase 3; Primary purpose: Treatment
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or modifications reported
mechanism_of_action: Bispecific T‑cell engager that binds DLL3 on SCLC cells and CD3 on T cells, redirecting T‑cell cytotoxicity to DLL3‑expressing tumor cells; engineered with an extended half‑life BiTE format.

title: Ipilimumab in Melanoma (NCT00324155)
description: Ipilimumab, a CTLA‑4 checkpoint inhibitor, was investigated in a Phase 3, randomized, double‑blind, placebo‑controlled trial (NCT00324155) for unresectable stage III/IV melanoma. The study enrolled approximately 400 patients, comparing ipilimumab 10 mg/kg every 3 weeks for four doses versus placebo, with overall survival as the primary endpoint and progression‑free survival, objective response rate, and safety as secondary endpoints. The trial met its primary endpoint, demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in overall survival. The sponsor was Bristol‑Myers Squibb; the trial received Breakthrough Therapy and Orphan Drug designations in the United States. The study began enrollment in 2008, completed recruitment in 2013, and final results were published in 2015.
oncology_drug_check: Melanoma – biologic (Ipilimumab)
development_status: The trial is completed, with study completion reported as 2013-10. The sponsor is Bristol-Myers Squibb. The trial and follow-up publications indicate that the trial met its primary endpoint, demonstrating a statistically significant overall survival benefit for ipilimumab + dacarbazine. Ipilimumab subsequently continued in development and is an approved CTLA-4 inhibitor for melanoma.
drug_indication: Ipilimumab (in this trial, MDX-010/BMS-734016) was tested for untreated, unresectable Stage III or IV melanoma; administered with dacarbazine chemotherapy in the trial.
mechanism_context: Ipilimumab is a CTLA-4 immune checkpoint inhibitor that potentiates anti-tumor T-cell responses by blocking CTLA-4-mediated inhibition of T-cell activation (immune-oncology agent).
progress_timeline_date: The registry lists Study Start as 2006-08; Primary Completion (Actual) as 2011-01; Study Completion (Actual) as 2013-10; and Enrollment (Actual) as 681 patients. Later publications report multi-year follow-up, including 4–5 year survival updates and pooled analyses.
trial_design: Phase 3, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, two-arm study (CA184-024 / NCT00324155). Arms: dacarbazine + ipilimumab (ipilimumab 10 mg/kg q3w ×4) vs dacarbazine + placebo. Primary endpoint was overall survival (OS); secondary endpoints included progression-free survival (PFS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety.
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Nivolumab, pembrolizumab, nivolumab + ipilimumab, nivolumab + relatlimab, BRAF/MEK inhibitor combinations
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements (patients were eligible regardless of LDH level and BRAF mutation status)
geographic_scope: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Germany, Spain, United Kingdom, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Norway, Portugal
key_competing_drugs_trials: Nivolumab, pembrolizumab, nivolumab + ipilimumab, nivolumab + relatlimab, BRAF/MEK inhibitor combinations
current_standard_of_care: PD‑1 inhibitors (nivolumab, pembrolizumab), PD‑1 + CTLA‑4 (nivolumab‑ipilimumab), PD‑1 + LAG‑3 (nivolumab‑relatlimab), and for BRAF V600‑mutant disease BRAF + MEK inhibitor combinations; T‑VEC is an option for select patients.
primary_endpoints: Overall survival (OS)
target_population: Adults ≥18 years with previously untreated unresectable stage IIIc (N3) or stage IV cutaneous melanoma; ECOG performance status 0-1; excluded patients with brain metastases, ocular or mucosal melanoma, and symptomatic autoimmune disease.
trial_progress_evidence: Results posted to ClinicalTrials.gov in 2014; primary results published in NEJM 2011; 5‑year survival update published in JCO 2015.
previous_phase_results_summary: Prior pivotal study showed ipilimumab 3 mg/kg monotherapy improved overall survival in previously treated metastatic melanoma; the benefit and immune‑related adverse‑event profile supported advancing a first‑line combination phase 3 trial.
secondary_endpoints: Progression‑free survival (PFS), objective/best overall response (BOR) and duration, disease control rate (DCR), time to BOR, survival rates at 1, 2, and 3 years, safety profile, health‑related quality of life (HRQoL)
expected_readout_date: Expected readout date unknown (trial completed; results published)
comparator_control_arm: Placebo + dacarbazine
study_design: Phase 3, randomized, double‑blind, placebo‑controlled, parallel‑group, multicenter study
safety_signals_modifications: Higher rates of grade 3/4 liver function test elevations with ipilimumab + dacarbazine; overall immune‑related adverse events consistent with prior ipilimumab studies; no drug‑related deaths or gastrointestinal perforations reported.
mechanism_of_action: Ipilimumab is an anti‑CTLA‑4 immune checkpoint inhibitor that blocks CTLA‑4, enhancing T‑cell activation and antitumor immune responses.

title: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda®) – Phase 3 trial NCT03765918 in resectable locally advanced head and neck squamous‑cell carcinoma
description: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda®) is being evaluated in the Phase 3 trial NCT03765918, a randomized, double‑blind study of neoadjuvant and adjuvant pembrolizumab versus standard surgery ± radiation in patients with resectable locally advanced head and neck squamous‑cell carcinoma (HNSCC). The trial targets adult patients with stage III‑IVB HNSCC suitable for curative intent surgery, requires PD‑L1 expression ≥1 % for eligibility, and assesses pembrolizumab as monotherapy in both neoadjuvant and adjuvant settings.
oncology_drug_check: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (MeSH:D001943); Biologic: Pembrolizumab
development_status: Merck has announced that KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab) met the primary endpoint of improved EFS when added perioperatively to standard care in resected locally advanced HNSCC. ClinicalTrials.gov lists primary completion on 2024-07-25 and an estimated study completion of 2026-09-10. Full detailed results, including efficacy and safety data, are expected to be published in press releases, conference presentations, and peer-reviewed publications.
drug_indication: The study drug is Pembrolizumab (KEYTRUDA®; MK-3475). The indication under study is perioperative (neoadjuvant + adjuvant/maintenance) pembrolizumab for resectable, locally advanced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) — specifically, newly diagnosed, stage III or IVA resectable LA HNSCC of the oropharynx, larynx, hypopharynx, or oral cavity.
mechanism_context: Pembrolizumab (KEYTRUDA) is a PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibitor (monoclonal antibody) that blocks PD-1 on T cells, releasing inhibitory signaling and enabling antitumor T-cell responses. Clinical use leverages anti-PD-1–mediated immune activation to enhance tumor control in multiple cancer types.
progress_timeline_date: The study started on 2018-12-17. Primary completion was on 2024-07-25, and the estimated study completion is 2026-09-10. The actual enrollment was 714 participants. A key milestone is that Merck reported KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab) met the trial’s primary endpoint of event-free survival (EFS) as a perioperative regimen in resected locally advanced HNSCC.
trial_design: The trial, KEYNOTE-689 (NCT03765918), is a randomized, open-label, active-controlled Phase 3 study with parallel assignment. Patients were randomized 1:1 to receive either pembrolizumab + standard of care (SOC) or SOC alone. The population includes treatment-naïve patients with newly diagnosed, stage III or IVA resectable locally advanced HNSCC suitable for curative-intent surgery, with an ECOG performance status of 0–1. Exclusions included T4B, N3, distant metastases, non-specified head/neck primaries, and prior anti–PD-1/PD-L1 therapy. Efficacy outcomes were assessed by tumor PD-L1 combined positive score (CPS) subgroups (≥10, ≥1, and all participants). The experimental arm received neoadjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV for 2 cycles (up to ~6 weeks) prior to surgery, followed by adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV for 15 cycles (up to ~45 weeks) plus investigator’s choice SOC adjuvant therapy (radiotherapy with or without cisplatin). The control arm received SOC (surgery ± adjuvant radiotherapy with or without cisplatin) without pembrolizumab. The primary endpoint is Event-free survival (EFS), evaluated sequentially in PD-L1 CPS ≥10, then CPS ≥1, then the overall population. Secondary endpoints include Overall survival (OS), major pathological response (mPR), pathological complete response (pCR), and safety/tolerability.
interim_analysis_results: Merck reported KEYNOTE-689 met the primary endpoint of EFS (perioperative pembrolizumab regimen vs SOC)
regulatory_designations: FDA Priority Review (Feb 25, 2025) for the perioperative regimen based on KEYNOTE-689 results
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Cetuximab (EGFR inhibitor, part of EXTREME regimen in HNSCC); Pembrolizumab is approved in R/M HNSCC settings
biomarker_requirements: Central PD-L1 testing on tumor tissue required for stratification (CPS analysis) but no minimum PD-L1 expression threshold required for enrollment; local p16 IHC testing is required for oropharyngeal cancers to determine HPV status; no specific mutation requirement
key_competing_drugs_trials: KEYNOTE-412 (pembrolizumab + chemoradiation in unresected LA HNSCC)
therapy_type: Neoadjuvant monotherapy (pembrolizumab) followed by adjuvant combination therapy (pembrolizumab + radiotherapy  cisplatin)
current_standard_of_care: Surgical resection followed by adjuvant radiotherapy alone or radiotherapy plus cisplatin (100 mg/m2 q3w) based on high-risk features (positive margin or extranodal extension)
primary_endpoints: Event-Free Survival (EFS) per RECIST 1.1 by blinded independent central review, tested hierarchically in PD-L1 CPS≥10, CPS≥1, and all participants
target_population: Adults ≥18 years with newly diagnosed, resectable, non-metastatic LA HNSCC eligible for primary surgery. Stage definitions include: Stage III oropharyngeal p16-positive T4 (N0-N2), or Stage III/IVA p16-negative oropharynx, or Stage III/IVA larynx/hypopharynx/oral cavity. ECOG 0–1; adequate organ function; evaluable disease; PD-L1 tissue submission; p16 testing for oropharyngeal
sponsor_developer: Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC (Merck & Co., Inc.)
trial_progress_evidence: Study started 2018-12-17; primary completion 2024-07-25; interim analysis showed EFS benefit and trial met its primary endpoint; FDA accepted and granted Priority Review of the perioperative regimen application in 2025
previous_phase_results_summary: A Phase II neoadjuvant pembrolizumab MISP study (NCT02296684) showed pathological treatment effects (≥10% tumor shrinkage) in 43% and clinical-to-pathologic downstaging in 48%, with no unexpected surgical delays/complications; prior KEYNOTE studies in R/M HNSCC (KN012, KN055, KN040, KN048) established activity and durability of pembrolizumab and relevance of PD-L1 CPS
secondary_endpoints: Major pathological response (mPR ≤10% residual invasive SCC) at surgery; overall survival (OS); pathological complete response (pCR); patient-reported outcomes (EORTC QLQ-C30 GHS/QoL and physical functioning; EORTC QLQ-H&N35 swallowing, speech, pain); safety (AEs, discontinuations due to AEs). Exploratory analyses include local-regional control, distant metastasis-free survival, EQ-5D-5L utilities, and biomarker correlations
comparator_control_arm: Standard-of-care surgery with adjuvant radiotherapy  cisplatin (no neoadjuvant therapy, no pembrolizumab)
study_design: Phase 3, randomized, open-label, active-controlled, parallel assignment; perioperative regimen with neoadjuvant pembrolizumab (2 cycles) followed by surgery and adjuvant pembrolizumab with standard-of-care radiotherapy  cisplatin versus standard-of-care surgery followed by radiotherapy  cisplatin alone; primary purpose: treatment; BICR for efficacy assessments; independent DMC with pre-specified interim analyses
safety_signals_modifications: No specific safety signals or protocol-driven safety modifications reported publicly; ongoing AE monitoring per CTCAE v4.0 with independent DMC oversight and 2 planned efficacy interims
mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized IgG4 monoclonal antibody that binds PD-1 and blocks interaction with its ligands PD-L1 and PD-L2, enhancing T-cell activity to facilitate tumor regression

title: Ivonescimab
description: Ivonescimab is an investigational biologic being evaluated in the global Phase III HARMONi trial for first‑line treatment of metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The study compares ivonescimab plus platinum‑doublet chemotherapy against pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy, with overall survival and progression‑free survival as primary endpoints.
oncology_drug_check: non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) – drug/biologic
development_status: Ivonescimab is in active clinical development. It is marketed and approved in China for at least one second-line NSCLC indication (NMPA May 2024) and is undergoing global Phase III evaluation across multiple settings within the HARMONi program. The program has reported positive trial signals, including an OS benefit in HARMONi-A, and has received FDA Fast Track designation for the HARMONi clinical trial setting.
drug_indication: Ivonescimab (SMT112) is being developed for multiple oncology indications. Key ongoing Phase III programs include first-line metastatic non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), specifically the HARMONi program (e.g., HARMONi-3). Ivonescimab has also received approval in China from the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) in May 2024 for the second-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC setting based on the HARMONi-A clinical trial.
mechanism_context: Ivonescimab is a tetravalent/engineered bispecific antibody that binds PD-1 and VEGF simultaneously (a dual PD-1/VEGF inhibitor). Its cooperative binding to PD-1 and VEGF enhances the blockade potency of both targets, providing a differentiated mechanism compared with single-target PD-1 or anti-VEGF agents.
progress_timeline_date: Ivonescimab received marketing approval from China's NMPA in May 2024 for a second-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC indication, based on HARMONi-A data. Multiple HARMONi Phase III programs are ongoing, with company presentations and abstracts cited for WCLC 2025 (September 6–9, 2025) and SITC 2025 (October 2025) for HARMONi data and overall survival readouts. The drug has also received Fast Track designation from the US FDA for the HARMONi setting. HARMONi-3, for first-line metastatic NSCLC, appears to be actively enrolling and conducting globally in 2024–2026, with data readouts and primary completion likely in the mid-to-late 2020s.
trial_design: The HARMONi program encompasses a family of randomized Phase III studies in various non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) settings. HARMONi-3 is a randomized Phase III study in first-line metastatic NSCLC, comparing ivonescimab plus platinum-doublet chemotherapy against pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy. Primary endpoints for HARMONi-3 include overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS). Other HARMONi trials (e.g., HARMONi-A, HARMONi-2, HARMONi-6, HARMONi-7) evaluate ivonescimab in different lines of treatment, histologies (squamous vs. non-squamous), or PD-L1 strata, and against various PD-1/PD-L1 comparators or placebo plus chemotherapy.
biomarker_requirements: PD‑L1 expression required (Tumor Proportion Score) and exclusion of actionable EGFR, ALK, ROS1, or BRAF V600E alterations
key_competing_drugs_trials: Pembrolizumab + chemotherapy
current_standard_of_care: Current standard of care unknown
primary_endpoints: Overall Survival (OS), Progression‑Free Survival (PFS)
target_population: Patients with first‑line metastatic (Stage IV) NSCLC, squamous or non‑squamous, no prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease; PD‑L1 TPS must be available; exclude tumors with actionable EGFR, ALK, ROS1, or BRAF V600E alterations; exclude small‑cell histology, symptomatic CNS metastases or CNS mets ≥1.5 cm, significant bleeding/coagulopathy, and radiographic major vessel encasement or cavitation.
sponsor_developer: Sponsor: Summit Therapeutics; Originator/Developer: Akeso Biopharma
trial_progress_evidence: Study start (actual) 2023‑10‑26; estimated primary completion 2027‑12‑31; estimated study completion 2028‑12‑31; planned enrollment 1080 patients.
previous_phase_results_summary: Phase II showed a 24‑month overall survival rate of 64.8% in 1L squamous NSCLC and a median overall survival of 22.5 months in 2L+ EGFR‑mutant NSCLC treated with ivonescimab‑based regimens.
secondary_endpoints: Response, safety
comparator_control_arm: Pembrolizumab + chemotherapy
study_design: Randomized, double‑blind, multiregional Phase 3; parallel assignment; quadruple masking
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or modifications reported
mechanism_of_action: Bispecific antibody that simultaneously targets PD‑1 and VEGF; engineered by fusing an anti‑PD‑1 scFv to a full anti‑VEGF monoclonal antibody, integrating anti‑angiogenesis and checkpoint blockade.

title: Dostarlimab (NCT03981796) Phase 3 Trial in Endometrial Cancer
description: A Phase 3, randomized, double‑blind trial (NCT03981796) evaluating dostarlimab in combination with carboplatin‑paclitaxel versus placebo with carboplatin‑paclitaxel in patients with primary advanced or recurrent endometrial cancer. Primary endpoints include progression‑free survival; secondary endpoints cover overall survival, objective response rate, and safety.
oncology_drug_check: Dostarlimab (drug/biologic) for endometrial cancer (MeSH: C04.588.322.250, ICD-10: C54.1)
development_status: The RUBY trial (NCT03981796) is currently 'Active, not recruiting', with planned long-term follow-up extending through its scheduled primary/study completion dates. Regulatory and clinical milestones include positive efficacy results reported in peer-reviewed publications and conference reports, with Part 1 demonstrating a statistically significant PFS benefit and interim OS results. These outcomes have supported regulatory actions, and Dostarlimab has received regulatory approvals for certain endometrial cancer indications.
drug_indication: Drug name: Dostarlimab (Jemperli; TSR-042). Indication relevant to this trial: treatment of patients with recurrent or primary advanced (Stage III/IV) endometrial cancer; RUBY evaluates dostarlimab in combination with carboplatin–paclitaxel followed by dostarlimab maintenance (and in Part 2, dostarlimab + niraparib maintenance) versus placebo plus carboplatin–paclitaxel and placebo maintenance.
mechanism_context: Dostarlimab (TSR-042; trade name Jemperli) is a humanized monoclonal antibody that binds programmed cell death protein 1 (PD-1) and blocks its interaction with PD-L1 and PD-L2, thereby restoring anti-tumor T-cell activity (PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibition).
progress_timeline_date: The study started on July 18, 2019, and has enrolled 785 participants. The recruitment status is 'Active, not recruiting'. The primary completion and study completion dates are listed as November 26, 2026, reflecting scheduled long-term follow-up, despite interim analyses and efficacy results already being published.
trial_design: This is a Phase 3, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter global study (RUBY; ENGOT–EN6/GOG–3031). It is a two-part study: Part 1 compares dostarlimab + carboplatin–paclitaxel (6 cycles) followed by dostarlimab maintenance (1000 mg q6w up to 3 years) versus placebo + carboplatin–paclitaxel followed by placebo maintenance. Part 2 evaluates dostarlimab + carboplatin–paclitaxel followed by dostarlimab + niraparib versus placebo + carboplatin–paclitaxel followed by placebo. Randomization is 1:1 for Part 1, with parallel assignment and quadruple blinding (participant, care provider, investigator, outcomes assessor). The patient population includes adult female participants (>=18 years) with histologically/cytologically proven recurrent or primary advanced endometrial cancer (Stage III/IV or first recurrence) meeting specific inclusion criteria (ECOG 0–1, adequate organ function, etc.). Primary endpoints (dual primary) are Progression-free survival (PFS; investigator assessment and BICR) and Overall survival (OS), with time frames up to approximately 6 years. Key secondary endpoints include Objective response rate (ORR), duration of response (DOR), disease control rate (DCR), safety (AEs/SAEs), and patient-reported outcomes (EQ–5D–5L, EORTC QLQ–C30/Q LQ–EN24).
interim_analysis_results: Second interim analysis demonstrated a statistically significant overall survival benefit (HR 0.69; 95% CI 0.54‑0.89; P=0.002) with greater benefit in dMMR/MSI‑H (HR 0.32) and a favorable trend in MMRp/MSS (HR 0.79); earlier analyses showed significant PFS benefit.
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Pembrolizumab, pembrolizumab + lenvatinib, niraparib (investigated in Part 2)
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
key_competing_drugs_trials: Pembrolizumab + carboplatin‑paclitaxel (NRG‑GY018/KEYNOTE‑868), Pembrolizumab + lenvatinib (KEYNOTE‑775), Durvalumab + carboplatin‑paclitaxel ± olaparib (DUO‑E)
current_standard_of_care: Platinum‑taxane chemotherapy remains the backbone for primary advanced/first recurrent endometrial cancer; immunotherapy combinations (e.g., pembrolizumab + chemotherapy, dostarlimab + chemotherapy for dMMR/MSI‑H) have become standard in defined populations.
primary_endpoints: Investigator-assessed progression‑free survival, overall survival
target_population: Adults with primary advanced (stage III or IV) or first recurrent endometrial cancer; broad histologies included; stratified by MMR/MSI status; no biomarker required for eligibility.
trial_progress_evidence: Initial PFS benefit reported in NEJM 2023; second interim analysis showed OS benefit (Ann Oncol 2024); FDA expanded indication in 2024 based on these results.
previous_phase_results_summary: No previous phase results summary available
secondary_endpoints: PFS by blinded independent central review, PFS2, overall response rate, duration of response, disease control rate, patient‑reported outcomes, safety/tolerability
comparator_control_arm: Placebo plus carboplatin‑paclitaxel (followed by placebo maintenance)
study_design: Global, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter, phase 3
safety_signals_modifications: Grade ≥3 and serious treatment‑emergent adverse events were ~12% higher with dostarlimab + chemo; immune‑related adverse events occurred in 40.7% vs 16.3%; discontinuation due to TEAEs 19.1% vs 8.1%; no new major safety signals reported.
mechanism_of_action: Dostarlimab is a PD-1–blocking antibody that binds to the PD-1 receptor and blocks its interaction with PD-L1 and PD-L2.

title: bomedemstat (MK-3543)
description: Bomedemstat (MK‑3543) is a lysine‑specific demethylase‑1 (LSD1) inhibitor being evaluated in a Phase 3 clinical trial (NCT06079879) for patients with myeloproliferative disorders. The study assesses efficacy and safety as monotherapy in a target population of adults with intermediate‑ or high‑risk disease who have failed prior therapy.
oncology_drug_check: Essential Thrombocythemia (ET) (MeSH: D004924); Intervention: Drug (bomedemstat)
development_status: Active development with Phase 3 initiated (start 2023-12-31); no suspensions/terminations shown in registry excerpt. Company literature and publications document ongoing clinical development and supporting preclinical/Phase 2 data.
drug_indication: Phase 3 trial NCT06079879 evaluates bomedemstat vs Best Available Therapy in adults with essential thrombocythemia who have an inadequate response to or are intolerant of hydroxyurea.
mechanism_context: Bomedemstat (MK-3543/IMG-7289) is an orally bioavailable, irreversible LSD1 inhibitor intended to modulate malignant hematopoiesis in MPNs by inhibiting LSD1-dependent proliferation/differentiation programs in hematopoietic stem/progenitor cells; preclinical and structural/PKPD data report low-nanomolar potency and specificity.
progress_timeline_date: Study start (actual) 2023-12-31; Primary completion (estimated) 2027-07-30. No additional definitive milestone dates were present in the registry excerpt.
trial_design: Phase 3, randomized, open-label, active-comparator-controlled (bomedemstat vs BAT), primary hypothesis: superiority for durable clinicohematologic response (DCHR); BAT options listed include anagrelide, busulfan, interferons, ruxolitinib.
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Hydroxyurea, pegylated interferon alfa, anagrelide, busulfan, ruxolitinib
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
geographic_scope: France, Germany, Hong Kong, Hungary, Netherlands, New Zealand, Poland, Portugal
key_competing_drugs_trials: Hydroxyurea, pegylated interferon alfa, anagrelide, busulfan, ruxolitinib
current_standard_of_care: Cytoreductive management of high‑risk ET commonly uses hydroxyurea or interferon; second‑line options include anagrelide, busulfan, and ruxolitinib, which are reflected in the BAT comparator.
primary_endpoints: Durable Clinicohematologic Response (DCHR) rate by Week 52, defined as confirmed platelet count ≤400×10^9/L; absence of WBC elevation >10×10^9/L; and no thrombotic or major hemorrhagic events or progression to MF/MDS/AML by Week 52
target_population: Adults with essential thrombocythemia (ET) per WHO 2016 criteria, centrally confirmed bone marrow fibrosis grade 0‑1, platelet count >450×10^9/L, ANC ≥0.75×10^9/L, inadequate response or intolerance to hydroxyurea, and up to three prior ET‑directed cytoreductive agents allowed.
trial_progress_evidence: Overall status Recruiting; Study Start (Actual) 2023-12-31; Primary completion (Estimated) 2027-07-30; Study completion (Estimated) 2028-08-18; Enrollment (Estimated) increased from 300 to 340 as of 2025-09-25; sites across multiple countries are recruiting or active.
previous_phase_results_summary: No previous phase results summary available
secondary_endpoints: Change from baseline in MFSAF v4.0 individual fatigue item score; Change from baseline in PROMIS Fatigue SF‑7a total fatigue score; Change from baseline in MFSAF v4.0 total symptom score; Duration of Clinicohematologic Response (DOCHR); Duration of Hematologic Remission (DOHR); Percentage of participants with thrombotic events; Number of participants who discontinue study treatment due to an adverse event
comparator_control_arm: Best Available Therapy (BAT), which may include anagrelide, busulfan, interferon alfa/pegylated interferon alfa‑2a/pegylated interferon alfa‑2b, or ruxolitinib
study_design: Phase 3; interventional; randomized; parallel assignment; open-label with single-masked outcomes assessor; active-comparator-controlled versus Best Available Therapy (BAT)
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or modifications reported
mechanism_of_action: Bomedemstat is an irreversible, covalent inhibitor of lysine-specific demethylase-1 (LSD1/KDM1A) that modifies the FAD cofactor in the LSD1 catalytic pocket, disrupting LSD1 activity and causing epigenetic reprogramming.

title: ifinatamab deruxtecan (MK-2400)
description: ifinatamab deruxtecan (MK-2400) is an investigational antibody‑drug conjugate that couples a humanized anti‑B7‑H3 IgG1 monoclonal antibody to a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload. It is being evaluated as a monotherapy in a Phase 3 clinical trial for prostate cancer.
oncology_drug_check: Metastatic Castration‑Resistant Prostate Cancer (mCRPC), Prostate Cancer, Prostatic Neoplasms (drug/biologic)
development_status: Overall status: Active Phase 3 development — the Phase 3 (IDeate‑Prostate01 / MK‑2400‑001) has been initiated and the first patient has been dosed according to company/news announcements; the program is in global clinical development with earlier phase 1/2 data reported in other tumor types (e.g., IDEATE‑LUNG01 phase 2) showing clinical activity. Ifinatamab deruxtecan has also received orphan‑drug designation for small cell lung cancer from multiple regulatory authorities (company disclosure).
drug_indication: Current Phase 3 indication under study: metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). The Phase 3 trial is for patients whose disease progressed following prior androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI) therapy.
mechanism_context: Ifinatamab deruxtecan (I‑DXd, MK‑2400) is an investigational, B7‑H3–directed antibody–drug conjugate (ADC) built on Daiichi Sankyo’s DXd platform: a humanized anti‑B7‑H3 IgG1 monoclonal antibody conjugated via a cleavable tetrapeptide linker to a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (an exatecan‑derived DXd payload). B7‑H3 is reported to be overexpressed in multiple cancers including metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), supporting it as a therapeutic target.
progress_timeline_date: First patient dosed / trial launch: announced mid‑June 2025 (press reports/press release noted first patient dosed around the Phase 3 initiation announcement). Enrollment target: ~1,440 patients (global). Estimated completion: news coverage indicates completion anticipated by June 2028 (reported estimate). Registry metadata: clinicaltrials.gov record NCT06925737 (last update posted 2025‑10‑20) lists Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC as responsible party/sponsor.
trial_design: Phase: Phase 3, randomized, global trial. Arms: Ifinatamab deruxtecan (I‑DXd) versus docetaxel with prednisone (chemotherapy comparator). Population: Patients with mCRPC after prior ARPI (per trial description/news); planned enrollment ~1,440 patients globally. Key endpoints: Co‑primary endpoints include overall survival (OS) and radiographic progression‑free survival (rPFS). Secondary endpoints include objective/response measures and safety. Randomization and comparator: Active comparator (docetaxel + prednisone); randomized design.
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements. Tumor tissue collection is required for screening; B7‑H3 expression is collected for stratification but not required for eligibility.
key_competing_drugs_trials: Docetaxel, Cabazitaxel, PARP inhibitors (e.g., olaparib, rucaparib) combined with ARPIs, PARP monotherapy in HRR‑mutated disease, radioligand therapy [177Lu]Lu‑PSMA‑617 (Pluvicto), radium‑223.
current_standard_of_care: In the post‑ARPI mCRPC setting, standard of care includes taxane chemotherapy (docetaxel, later cabazitaxel), ARPI sequencing, PARP inhibitors for HRR‑mutated disease, and radioligand therapies such as [177Lu]Lu‑PSMA‑617 and radium‑223 for appropriate patients.
primary_endpoints: Overall survival (OS), radiographic progression‑free survival (rPFS)
target_population: Adult males with histologically confirmed metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have progressed on androgen deprivation therapy and have received 1–2 prior ARPIs. Exclusions include prior taxane chemotherapy for mCRPC, history of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis requiring steroids, and uncontrolled cardiovascular disease.
sponsor_developer: Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC (Merck & Co., Inc.). Funding support and collaboration with Daiichi Sankyo Company, Limited.
trial_progress_evidence: Status listed as Recruiting/Active; enrollment ongoing according to the Veeva study page.
line_of_therapy: Post‑ARPI, taxane‑naïve (1–2 prior ARPIs allowed) in the mCRPC setting.
previous_phase_results_summary: Preliminary results from the first‑in‑human Phase 1/2 IDeate‑PanTumor01 study showed antitumor activity and manageable safety across solid tumors, including heavily pretreated mCRPC; in ES‑SCLC, I‑DXd achieved a 48.2% ORR with median PFS of 4.9 months.
secondary_endpoints: Time to first subsequent therapy, objective response, duration of response, time to pain progression, time to PSA progression, PSA response, time to first symptomatic skeletal‑related event, safety and tolerability.
comparator_control_arm: Docetaxel 75 mg/m² q3w plus prednisone/prednisolone (per label).
study_design: Phase 3, randomized (1:1), open‑label, parallel assignment.
safety_signals_modifications: Risk of interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis; patients instructed to report symptoms promptly. No protocol modifications reported.
mechanism_of_action: Ifinatamab deruxtecan (I‑DXd) is a humanized anti‑B7‑H3 IgG1 antibody linked via a cleavable linker to a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (DXd). Binding to B7‑H3 on tumor cells leads to internalization and release of DXd, killing the cell and potentially exerting a by‑stander effect on neighboring B7‑H3‑negative cells.

title: NCT05578976
description: NCT05578976 – Phase 3 EPCORE® FL-1 trial of epcoritamab + rituximab + lenalidomide versus rituximab + lenalidomide in relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma.
oncology_drug_check: Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma; oncology drug
development_status: At the time of the principal registry snapshot, EPCORE FL-1 was an active, ongoing Phase 3 randomized trial, with global enrollment opening in 2022. As of November 18, 2025, epcoritamab-bysp (EPKINLY) in combination with rituximab + lenalidomide was announced as FDA-approved for relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma in the U.S. This indicates the drug has moved from clinical development into regulatory approval for this combination/indication in the U.S. The trial continued/continues to provide data and follow-up per registry timelines, with potential for post-approval trials or labeling updates.
drug_indication: The drug is Epcoritamab (trade name: EPKINLY / epcoritamab-bysp). The combination evaluated in this trial is epcoritamab + rituximab + lenalidomide versus rituximab + lenalidomide (R2). The indication for this trial is relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma (grades 1–3a) in subjects with at least 1 prior line of anti-lymphoma therapy.
mechanism_context: Epcoritamab (epcoritamab-bysp / EPKINLY) is a subcutaneously administered, bispecific CD3xCD20 T-cell–engaging antibody. It binds CD3 on T cells and CD20 on B cells to activate and redirect T cells to kill malignant CD20+ B cells, inducing T-cell–mediated cytotoxicity of B-cell malignancies. Clinical reports emphasize potent anti-tumor activity in B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma with an expected safety profile including cytokine release syndrome (generally low grade) and immune-mediated events. The biological context involves targeting CD20-positive B cells (including follicular lymphoma cells) and relying on the recruitment and activation of patient T cells. It is given subcutaneously to enable outpatient administration and is often evaluated in combination with anti-CD20 rituximab and immunomodulatory agent lenalidomide (R2) to potentially deepen responses.
progress_timeline_date: The actual study start date was 2022-09-20. Approximately 549 participants were enrolled. The estimated primary completion and study completion dates are both December 2029. An additional milestone is the U.S. FDA approval of epcoritamab-bysp in combination with rituximab and lenalidomide for relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma, reported by AbbVie on November 18, 2025.
trial_design: The official study title is "A Phase 3, Open-Label Study to Evaluate Safety and Efficacy of Epcoritamab in Combination With Rituximab and Lenalidomide (R2) Compared to R2 in Subjects With Relapsed or Refractory Follicular Lymphoma (EPCORE FL-1)." It is a Phase 3, randomized, open-label, multicenter, global trial. The experimental arm involves subcutaneous epcoritamab + rituximab + lenalidomide (epcoritamab + R2), while the control arm uses rituximab + lenalidomide (R2) alone. Key inclusion criteria include adults with histologically confirmed classic follicular lymphoma (grades 1–3a), stage II–IV, CD20+ tumor, who are relapsed or refractory after at least one prior line of anti-lymphoma therapy. The primary efficacy endpoint is progression-free survival (PFS) by blinded independent central review. Secondary endpoints include overall response rate (ORR), complete response (CR) or complete metabolic response (CMR) rates, overall survival (OS), duration of response (DOR), safety/tolerability, and exploratory pharmacodynamic/pharmacokinetic assessments.
interim_analysis_results: Pre‑planned interim analysis met both primary endpoints; the triplet reduced risk of death or progression by 79% versus R2 (HR 0.21, P<.0001) and showed a significant ORR benefit; no new safety signals reported.
regulatory_designations: Breakthrough Therapy designation (epcoritamab + R2 combination in R/R lymphoma ≥1 prior line)
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Mosunetuzumab, Axicabtagene ciloleucel, Tisagenlecleucel, Lisocabtagene maraleucel, Tazemetostat, Odronextamab
biomarker_requirements: Histologically confirmed classic FL (grades 1‑3a); CD20‑positive tumor on biopsy; PET‑avid disease; no evidence of histologic transformation; no specific genomic biomarker requirements.
geographic_scope: North America, South America, Europe, Africa, Asia, Australia
key_competing_drugs_trials: Mosunetuzumab (GO29781), Axicabtagene ciloleucel (ZUMA‑5), Tisagenlecleucel (ELARA), Lisocabtagene maraleucel (TRANSCEND‑FL), Tazemetostat, Odronextamab (ELM‑2)
current_standard_of_care: R2 (rituximab + lenalidomide) is an approved second‑ or later‑line regimen for follicular lymphoma; other options in later lines include bispecific antibodies, CAR‑T cell therapies, and tazemetostat.
primary_endpoints: Progression‑free survival (PFS) by independent review committee; Best overall response (BOR) rate
target_population: Adults with relapsed/refractory classic follicular lymphoma (grades 1‑3a), stage II‑IV, who have received at least one prior anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody plus chemotherapy regimen; ECOG 0‑2; creatinine clearance ≥50 mL/min; ≥1 measurable lesion per Lugano 2014; CD20‑positive tumor; PET‑avid disease; no histologic transformation.
trial_progress_evidence: Study started on 2022‑09‑20; Arm B was closed after establishing 48 mg as the recommended dose; actual enrollment reached 549; interim analysis showed significant efficacy leading to sBLA acceptance in July 2025 and FDA approval in November 2025; primary completion remains projected for 2029‑12.
line_of_therapy: Second line and beyond (≥1 prior anti‑CD20 + chemotherapy regimen)
previous_phase_results_summary: In EPCORE NHL‑1 (phase 1/2), epcoritamab monotherapy showed an overall response rate of 82% across B‑cell NHL and 90% ORR with 50% CR in a small R/R FL cohort. In EPCORE NHL‑2 (phase 1b/2), epcoritamab + R2 achieved 98% ORR (n=104) with 87% complete metabolic response and primarily low‑grade CRS.
secondary_endpoints: Complete response (CR) rate, Overall survival, Minimal residual disease (MRD) negativity rate, Change in FACT‑Lym scale, Investigator‑assessed PFS, Investigator‑assessed BOR, Investigator‑assessed CR, Duration of CR, Time to disease progression, Event‑free survival, Other patient‑reported outcomes (e.g., time to next anti‑lymphoma therapy)
comparator_control_arm: Rituximab + Lenalidomide (R2)
study_design: Phase 3; randomized; open‑label; parallel assignment; treatment purpose; arms: Experimental epcoritamab + R2 (Dose A and Dose B, with Dose B closed); Active comparator: R2 alone
safety_signals_modifications: No new safety signals at interim; cytokine release syndrome (CRS) was mostly low grade with step‑up dosing; FDA label includes boxed warning for serious CRS and ICANS; protocol amendment closed Arm B and set recommended epcoritamab dose at 48 mg.
mechanism_of_action: Epcoritamab is a subcutaneous CD3×CD20 bispecific antibody that redirects T cells to kill CD20‑positive B cells. Rituximab is an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody that mediates cell‑dependent cytotoxicity. Lenalidomide is an immunomodulatory agent that enhances T‑cell and NK‑cell function and inhibits angiogenesis.

title: Belantamab mafodotin
description: Belantamab mafodotin is an anti‑BCMA antibody‑drug conjugate investigated in multiple myeloma. The referenced study (NCT04484623) is a Phase 2 trial evaluating belantamab mafodotin in combination with pomalidomide and dexamethasone in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients.
oncology_drug_check: Multiple Myeloma (C90.0); Drug/Biologic (Belantamab Mafodotin)
development_status: Belantamab mafodotin was initially FDA‑approved (accelerated) in 2020 for heavily pretreated RRMM but the U.S. approval was withdrawn following negative phase 3 data (DREAMM‑3) reported in 2022–2023. However, combination trials (including DREAMM‑7 and DREAMM‑8) subsequently reported positive results (statistically significant and clinically meaningful PFS benefit for BPd vs PVd in DREAMM‑8 as reported at major conferences in 2024 and further publications in 2025). These positive combination results prompted renewed regulatory activity and region-specific approvals/reviews in 2024–2025. The agent’s development has therefore transitioned from a withdrawn monotherapy indication toward development and regulatory review in combination regimens.
drug_indication: Belantamab mafodotin (trade name Blenrep when marketed) is indicated for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM). The DREAMM-8 trial specifically tests belantamab in combination with pomalidomide + dexamethasone (BPd) in patients with RRMM who have received prior lines of therapy.
mechanism_context: Belantamab mafodotin (Blenrep) is an antibody–drug conjugate (ADC) that targets B‑cell maturation antigen (BCMA) on plasma cells. The ADC delivers a cytotoxic microtubule-disrupting payload (monomethyl auristatin F, MMAF) into BCMA-expressing myeloma cells; cell killing occurs via internalization and intracellular release of the toxin and also through Fc-mediated immune effector functions (ADCC/ADCP). BCMA is highly expressed on late-stage B cells and malignant plasma cells in multiple myeloma, making it a validated antigen for targeted therapies. Ocular toxicity (corneal epithelial events) is a characteristic and dose-limiting toxicity of the mafodotin ADC class and has been an important safety consideration and dose-modification strategy in belantamab trials.
progress_timeline_date: The trial identifier is NCT04484623 (DREAMM-8). Enrollment began around 2021, with some summaries listing July 2021 as an early listing date. Key milestones include the reporting of DREAMM-8 results in major conference abstracts (ASCO/JCO/ASCO presentations) in 2024, showing a progression-free survival (PFS) benefit for BPd vs PVd. Patient-reported outcomes were published in Lancet Haematol in November 2025. Belantamab mafodotin received accelerated regulatory recognition (FDA approval in 2020), was withdrawn from the US market after failure to meet endpoints in DREAMM-3 (withdrawal action in 2022), and later combination-trial data (including DREAMM-7/DREAMM-8) produced positive results leading to renewed regulatory interest and regional approvals/reviews in 2024–2025. The ClinicalTrials.gov entry for NCT04484623 serves as the authoritative source for actual/estimated study start, primary completion, and study completion dates.
trial_design: DREAMM-8 (NCT04484623) is a randomized, open-label, phase 3 trial comparing belantamab mafodotin + pomalidomide + dexamethasone (BPd) versus bortezomib + pomalidomide + dexamethasone (PVd) in patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma. The patient population includes RRMM patients with at least one prior line of therapy. The study arms are Arm A: belantamab mafodotin + pomalidomide + dexamethasone (BPd) and a comparator arm: bortezomib + pomalidomide + dexamethasone (PVd). The primary efficacy endpoint reported was progression-free survival (PFS). Secondary endpoints included overall response rate (ORR), overall survival (OS), safety/tolerability (including ocular events), and patient‑reported outcomes.
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Idecabtagene vicleucel, Ciltacabtagene autoleucel, Teclistamab, Elranatamab, Daratumumab, Isatuximab
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
key_competing_drugs_trials: Idecabtagene vicleucel (Abecma), Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti), Teclistamab (Tecvayli), Elranatamab (Elrexfio), Daratumumab‑based regimens, Isatuximab‑based regimens
current_standard_of_care: Current RRMM management includes BCMA‑directed CAR‑T and bispecific antibodies (ide‑cel, cilta‑cel, teclistamab, elranatamab) and anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibodies combined with IMiD/PI regimens.
primary_endpoints: Progression‑Free Survival (PFS)
target_population: Patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma who have received at least one prior line of therapy including a lenalidomide‑containing regimen and have documented disease progression.
trial_progress_evidence: Primary completion occurred on 2024‑01‑29; positive topline results were announced in March 2024 and published in Blood Advances with details on ocular event management.
line_of_therapy: ≥2nd line (patients with at least one prior therapy including lenalidomide)
previous_phase_results_summary: Earlier Phase 1/2 DREAMM‑6 studies evaluated belantamab mafodotin with lenalidomide‑dexamethasone, showing safety and activity that supported the design of the Phase 3 DREAMM‑8 trial.
secondary_endpoints: Secondary endpoints unknown
comparator_control_arm: PVd (pomalidomide + bortezomib + dexamethasone)
study_design: Phase 3, multicentre, open‑label, randomized, 2‑arm interventional study
safety_signals_modifications: Ocular adverse events (keratopathy) were managed with dose delays; longer delays were associated with maintained progression‑free survival.
mechanism_of_action: Belantamab mafodotin (belamaf) is an afucosylated anti‑BCMA IgG1 antibody‑drug conjugate linked via a non‑cleavable maleimidocaproyl (MC) linker to monomethyl auristatin F (MMAF). It binds BCMA on myeloma cells, is internalized, and the MC linker releases MMAF in lysosomes, disrupting microtubules, causing G2/M arrest and apoptosis. Afucosylation enhances FcγRIIIa engagement, augmenting ADCC and ADCP.

title: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda®)
description: Phase 3 trial (NCT03924895) evaluating pembrolizumab monotherapy in muscle‑invasive bladder cancer patients who are ineligible for cisplatin‑based chemotherapy.
oncology_drug_check: muscle‑invasive bladder cancer – drug
development_status: The drug is in a Phase 3 interventional study. The trial has an actual enrollment of 595 participants. The primary completion date was 2025-06-06, and the estimated study completion date is 2027-12-15. Secondary sites and updates are recorded on the trial record, which also contains status information and date stamps for first posted and last update.
drug_indication: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda®, investigational code MK-3475) is being evaluated as perioperative (neoadjuvant ± adjuvant) therapy in cisplatin-ineligible patients with muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma (muscle-invasive bladder cancer, MIBC) in the randomized phase 3 study KEYNOTE-905 / MK-3475-905.
mechanism_context: Pembrolizumab (KEYTRUDA) is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-1 interaction with PD-L1/PD-L2, thereby reactivating T cells and antitumor immune responses. This immunologic mechanism is relevant in muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC), a molecularly diverse disease, where immune checkpoint blockade can restore anti-tumor immunity in tumors that express PD-1/PD-L1.
progress_timeline_date: The study started on 2019-07-24. The primary completion date was 2025-06-06, and the estimated study completion date is 2027-12-15. The actual enrollment for the trial is 595 participants.
trial_design: This is a global, randomized phase III perioperative study (KEYNOTE-905 / MK-3475-905) comparing perioperative pembrolizumab plus radical cystectomy with pelvic lymph node dissection (RC+PLND) versus RC+PLND alone in cisplatin-ineligible muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC) patients. Some sources indicate additional experimental arms that include enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab + surgery. Example arm descriptions include: Pembrolizumab + Surgery (Experimental), where participants receive 3 preoperative cycles of pembrolizumab, followed by standard of care surgery, followed by 14 cycles of postoperative pembrolizumab (each cycle is 21 days); Surgery alone (Active Comparator), which involves standard of care RC+PLND; and an Enfortumab Vedotin + Pembrolizumab + Surgery (Experimental) arm, involving a preoperative combination followed by a postoperative combination and then pembrolizumab monotherapy cycles. The patient population consists of cisplatin-ineligible patients with histologically confirmed urothelial carcinoma staged as muscle-invasive (e.g., cT2-T4aN0M0 or certain node-positive configurations per protocol), with predominant (≥50%) urothelial histology and planned radical cystectomy with pelvic lymph node dissection. Primary and secondary endpoints are listed on the ClinicalTrials.gov study record, with the primary completion date having been reached on 2025-06-06. Known identifiers include MK-3475-905 and KEYNOTE-905, along with other registry identifiers such as EV-303, EudraCT, and jRCT numbers.
interim_analysis_results: No interim analysis results posted on ClinicalTrials.gov; external reporting indicates a positive topline survival benefit for the enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab arm versus surgery alone.
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
key_competing_drugs_trials: Adjuvant nivolumab (CheckMate 274), Atezolizumab neoadjuvant monotherapy (ABACUS), Perioperative enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab (KEYNOTE-905/EV-303), Durvalumab‑based perioperative approaches (NIAGARA)
current_standard_of_care: For cisplatin‑ineligible but surgically fit patients, radical cystectomy with pelvic lymph node dissection is standard; adjuvant nivolumab is used for high‑risk residual disease. Bladder‑preserving trimodality therapy is an alternative in selected cases.
primary_endpoints: Event‑Free Survival (EFS) between Arm A (pembrolizumab) vs Arm B (surgery alone); Event‑Free Survival (EFS) between Arm C (enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab) vs Arm B
target_population: Adults with clinically nonmetastatic muscle‑invasive bladder cancer (MIBC) (≥50% urothelial histology), stages cT2–T4aN0M0 or T1–T4aN1M0, ECOG 0–2, eligible for radical cystectomy + pelvic lymph node dissection, and cisplatin‑ineligible (e.g., creatinine clearance 30–59 mL/min or ECOG 2) or who decline cisplatin.
sponsor_developer: Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC (Merck & Co., Inc.)
trial_progress_evidence: Study Start (Actual) 2019-07-24; Primary Completion (Actual) 2025-06-06; Study Completion (Estimated) 2027-12-15; Enrollment (Actual) 595.
line_of_therapy: Perioperative (neoadjuvant and adjuvant) around curative‑intent surgery
previous_phase_results_summary: The neoadjuvant PURE‑01 study of pembrolizumab monotherapy in MIBC reported a pathological complete response rate of ~38.5% (ypT0N0) and encouraging event‑free survival, providing rationale for the perioperative Phase 3 evaluation.
secondary_endpoints: Pathologic complete response (pCR) rate, Overall Survival (OS), Event‑Free Survival (EFS) time frames up to ~6.75 years, OS up to ~7.6 years, pCR up to ~5.7 years
comparator_control_arm: Radical cystectomy (RC) + pelvic lymph node dissection (PLND) alone (observation thereafter)
study_design: Randomized, parallel‑assignment, open‑label, Phase 3, 3‑arm perioperative trial
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals noted on the registry; protocol amendments include removal of PD‑L1 CPS outcome measures (Amendment 5) and shifting pCR from primary to secondary (Amendment 8).
mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized IgG4‑kappa monoclonal antibody that binds the PD‑1 receptor on T cells and blocks its interaction with PD‑L1/PD‑L2, thereby releasing inhibition of the PD‑1 pathway and enhancing anti‑tumor T‑cell activity.

title: Belantamab mafodotin
description: Belantamab mafodotin is an anti‑BCMA antibody‑drug conjugate evaluated in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma. The trial (NCT04246047) tests the drug in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone against a daratumumab‑bortezomib‑dexamethasone regimen.
oncology_drug_check: relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma (MeSH: D009103) (drug/biologic)
development_status: The development of belantamab mafodotin has a complex regulatory history. GSK initiated the withdrawal of US marketing authorization for Blenrep on 2022-11-22. However, more recent regulatory material (dated 2025-10-23) indicates an approval action and cites DREAMM-7 efficacy results, including a Boxed Warning for ocular toxicity. As of the last ClinicalTrials.gov update (2024-10-24) and the recent regulatory announcement (2025-10-23), the drug is actively undergoing Phase 3 evaluation and regulatory discussion, with indications of approval.
drug_indication: Belantamab mafodotin (also known as Blenrep or belantamab mafodotin-blmf) is developed for the treatment of relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma. The DREAMM-7 trial (NCT04246047) specifically compared belantamab mafodotin in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone (BVd) against daratumumab, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (DVd).
mechanism_context: Belantamab mafodotin is an anti-BCMA antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) that targets B-cell maturation antigen (BCMA) on multiple myeloma cells and delivers a cytotoxic payload. A notable adverse effect is ocular toxicity, specifically corneal epithelium changes and keratopathy, which requires clinical monitoring.
progress_timeline_date: The ClinicalTrials.gov record for NCT04246047 was last updated on 2024-10-24. A regulatory summary page from the FDA, which includes approval text and DREAMM-7 efficacy summary, is dated 2025-10-23. Historically, GSK initiated the withdrawal of US marketing authorization for Blenrep on 2022-11-22. Specific study start, primary completion, and study completion dates were not explicitly provided in the initial extraction but are typically available in the full ClinicalTrials.gov record.
trial_design: The DREAMM-7 trial (NCT04246047) is a Phase 3, randomized, open-label study sponsored by GlaxoSmithKline. Patients were randomized 1:1 to receive either belantamab mafodotin + bortezomib + dexamethasone (BVd) or daratumumab + bortezomib + dexamethasone (DVd). The study population included adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who had received prior therapy, excluding those refractory or intolerant to daratumumab or bortezomib, or with prior BCMA-directed therapy. Efficacy endpoints for regulatory evaluation included progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS). The median PFS was 31.3 months in the BVd arm versus 10.4 months in the DVd arm (HR 0.31). The median OS was not reached in the BVd arm versus 35.7 months in the DVd arm (HR 0.49).
interim_analysis_results: First interim analysis showed a statistically significant PFS benefit for B‑Vd vs D‑Vd (≈23‑month median PFS improvement). Second interim analysis reported a statistically significant OS improvement and >2‑fold higher CR and MRD‑negativity rates for B‑Vd.
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
geographic_scope: Australia, Belgium, Brazil, Canada, China, Czechia, France, Germany, Greece, Israel, Italy, Japan, Netherlands, New Zealand, Poland, Russia, South Korea, Spain, United Kingdom, United States
key_competing_drugs_trials: Daratumumab + bortezomib + dexamethasone (D‑Vd)
current_standard_of_care: Daratumumab + bortezomib + dexamethasone (D‑Vd) is widely accepted standard of care for RRMM after ≥1 prior line of therapy
primary_endpoints: Progression‑free Survival (PFS) by Independent Review Committee per IMWG 2016 criteria
target_population: Adults (≥18) with RRMM after ≥1 prior line, measurable disease per IMWG, ECOG 0‑2, adequate organ function; excludes patients intolerant/refractory to daratumumab or bortezomib, prior anti‑BCMA therapy, corneal epithelial disease, prior allogeneic SCT, and significant uncontrolled comorbidities.
trial_progress_evidence: Active, not recruiting; Primary Completion 2023-10-02; Study Completion (estimated) 2026-06-19; Last update posted 2024-10-24; protocol amendment added interim analyses for efficacy.
previous_phase_results_summary: Prior Phase 1/2 studies showed belantamab mafodotin monotherapy ORR ~60% with median PFS ~12 months in heavily pre‑treated RRMM; Phase 2 (DREAMM‑2) demonstrated comparable efficacy at 2.5 mg/kg with lower adverse events, informing dose selection for this Phase 3 trial.
secondary_endpoints: CR rate, ORR, CBR, DoR, TTR, TTP, OS, PFS2, MRD negativity rate, safety labs, ocular findings, PK, ADA incidence, PRO‑CTCAE, HRQoL (EORTC QLQ‑C30, EORTC IL‑52, EQ‑5D‑3L)
comparator_control_arm: Daratumumab + bortezomib + dexamethasone (D‑Vd)
study_design: Phase 3, interventional, randomized, open‑label, parallel assignment, two arms
safety_signals_modifications: Protocol mandates baseline and Q3W ophthalmic exams up to dose 6, then quarterly if stable; KVA‑based dose modifications for corneal events; added interim analysis for efficacy; standard hematologic AE management for bortezomib/dexamethasone.
mechanism_of_action: Humanized anti‑BCMA antibody‑drug conjugate that delivers cytotoxic cys‑mcMMAF to BCMA‑expressing myeloma cells, engages ADCC/ADCP and induces immunogenic cell death

title: Dostarlimab
description: Dostarlimab (dostarlimab‑gxly) is an anti‑PD‑1 monoclonal antibody approved for dMMR/MSI‑H endometrial cancer and is being evaluated in a Phase 3 peri‑operative trial (NCT05855200) for untreated T4N0 or stage III dMMR/MSI‑H resectable colon cancer.
oncology_drug_check: dMMR/MSI-H resectable colon cancer – PD-1 blocking antibody (biologic)
development_status: Dostarlimab (Jemperli) is an approved anti-PD-1 agent with FDA approvals for dMMR solid tumors and certain endometrial cancer indications. It is part of GSK's expanded immuno-oncology program (AZUR program) for dMMR/MSI-H colorectal cancer and other tumor types. The FDA has granted accelerated approval for dostarlimab-gxly in dMMR advanced solid tumors based on the GARNET trial and approved a companion diagnostic. GSK has also obtained Breakthrough Therapy Designation for locally advanced dMMR/MSI-H rectal cancer. The NCT05855200 trial is an active GSK Phase 3 perioperative study, with its record last updated on 2025-08-08, indicating ongoing development.
drug_indication: Dostarlimab (Jemperli; dostarlimab-gxly) is being evaluated perioperatively for untreated T4N0 or stage III, dMMR/MSI-H resectable colon cancer in a Phase 3 randomized, open-label trial (NCT05855200 / GSK AZUR-2 registrational context).
mechanism_context: Dostarlimab is an anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody (PD-1 blocker) that enhances anti-tumor immunity; dMMR/MSI-H colon cancers are biologically primed for response to PD-1 blockade due to high neoantigen load and mismatch repair deficiency.
progress_timeline_date: The ClinicalTrials.gov record NCT05855200 was last updated on 2025-08-08. The verbatim source excerpts did not contain explicit Start Date, Enrollment, or Estimated Completion Dates, but the trial is a current GSK registrational Phase 3 peri-operative trial, indicating active development in 2024-2025.
trial_design: The trial NCT05855200 is a Phase 3, randomized, parallel-assignment, open-label interventional study with a primary purpose of treatment. It compares perioperative dostarlimab (pre- and post-surgery) against a standard-of-care perioperative strategy (FOLFOX or CAPEOX) or observation post-surgery. The target population is untreated T4N0 or stage III dMMR/MSI-H resectable colon cancer. Specific primary and secondary endpoints were not explicitly captured in the provided verbatim excerpts, but are typically present on the ClinicalTrials.gov page.
biomarker_requirements: Tumor must demonstrate dMMR or MSI‑H
geographic_scope: Argentina, Australia, Belgium, Brazil, Canada, China, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Italy, Japan, Korea (Republic of), Mexico, Netherlands, Norway, Panama, Portugal, Spain, Sweden, Taiwan, Turkey, United Kingdom, United States
key_competing_drugs_trials: Atezolizumab (ATOMIC), Avelumab (POLEM), Nivolumab + Ipilimumab (NICHE-2)
current_standard_of_care: Adjuvant oxaliplatin‑based chemotherapy (CAPEOX or FOLFOX) for stage III disease; observation or CAPEOX/FOLFOX for stage II; adjuvant immunotherapy is not routinely recommended outside trials.
primary_endpoints: Event-free survival (EFS)
target_population: Adults (≥18) with previously untreated, pathologically confirmed, resectable colon adenocarcinoma that is clinically T4N0 or Stage III, ECOG 0‑1, radiologically evaluable disease, dMMR/MSI‑H; no symptomatic bowel obstruction, no prior therapy, no metastatic disease, no recent major surgery, no ILD/pneumonitis, no unstable liver disease, no prior allogeneic transplant, not pregnant or breastfeeding, and no severe allergy to study drugs.
trial_progress_evidence: Recruiting status reported as active‑recruiting across many global sites; last update posted Aug 2025 on ClinicalTrials.gov; interim EFS analysis planned ~12 months before final analysis.
previous_phase_results_summary: Neoadjuvant immunotherapy has shown promising efficacy in early‑stage dMMR colon cancer; dostarlimab demonstrated favorable benefit‑risk in dMMR locally advanced rectal cancer; NICHE‑2 (phase II) reported high pathological response rates in locally advanced dMMR colon cancers, supporting progression to this phase 3 peri‑operative study.
secondary_endpoints: Pathological response, overall survival (OS), safety, patient‑reported outcomes (HRQoL)
comparator_control_arm: Standard of care—surgery followed by adjuvant FOLFOX or CAPEOX for 3–6 months or watch‑and‑wait/observation per physician discretion
study_design: Phase 3, randomized, open-label, parallel assignment
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or protocol modifications reported
mechanism_of_action: Dostarlimab is an anti‑PD‑1 immune checkpoint inhibitor that blocks PD‑1, enhancing the immune system's ability to recognize and attack cancer cells.

title: Niraparib + Pembrolizumab Maintenance Trial (NCT04475939)
description: Niraparib + pembrolizumab (NCT04475939) is a GSK‑sponsored clinical trial in NSCLC patients receiving maintenance therapy. It is a combination PARP‑inhibitor regimen; the trial phase is not identified as Phase 3, making it only loosely relevant to the request for Phase 3 oncology trials.
oncology_drug_check: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer; Drug/Biologic
development_status: The study is a Phase 3 trial. It has reached its actual primary completion date (2025-02-26) but no results have been posted on ClinicalTrials.gov as of the last public record scrape. The trial is currently 'Active, not recruiting'. Earlier phase-2 clinical data for niraparib + pembrolizumab in advanced NSCLC showed clinical activity, which supported the progression to this phase-3 study.
drug_indication: The drugs under study are niraparib (a PARP inhibitor) and pembrolizumab (an anti-PD-1). The indication is maintenance therapy for participants with advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung cancer (stage IIIB/IIIC or IV) whose disease remained stable or responded (SD/PR/CR) after first-line platinum-based chemotherapy with pembrolizumab.
mechanism_context: Niraparib is a PARP inhibitor that targets single-strand DNA break repair, exploiting homologous recombination deficiency in tumors. Pembrolizumab is an anti-PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibitor that reactivates anti-tumor T-cell responses. The combination is rationalized by preclinical and early clinical data suggesting PARP inhibitors can increase tumor DNA damage/neoantigen load and activate innate immune signaling pathways, potentially sensitizing tumors to PD-1 blockade. Phase-2 clinical data of niraparib + pembrolizumab in advanced NSCLC showed clinical activity, supporting its evaluation in the randomized phase-3 ZEAL-1L study.
progress_timeline_date: The study started on 2020-10-26. The actual primary completion date was 2025-02-26, and the estimated study completion date is 2026-02-06. As of the last update on 2025-09-29, the recruitment status is 'Active, not recruiting'.
trial_design: This is a Phase 3, multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-assignment, triple-masked study. The experimental arm involves niraparib plus pembrolizumab, while the comparator arm uses matching placebo plus pembrolizumab. The primary hypotheses focus on the benefit of niraparib plus pembrolizumab versus placebo plus pembrolizumab for Progression-Free Survival (PFS) and Overall Survival (OS). The study population includes participants with stage IIIB/IIIC or IV NSCLC who achieved stable disease, partial response, or complete response after standard-of-care first-line platinum-based induction chemotherapy with pembrolizumab. A total of 666 participants were enrolled.
biomarker_requirements: No PD‑L1 requirement for eligibility; exclusion of "known targetable driver alteration" (e.g., oncogenic drivers with approved targeted therapy). Tumor tissue submission required. PD‑L1 is used only for subgroup analyses, not as an inclusion criterion.
key_competing_drugs_trials: No key competing drugs/trials identified
current_standard_of_care: First‑line platinum‑based chemotherapy combined with pembrolizumab, followed by maintenance pembrolizumab (the study enrolls after completion of 4–6 cycles of 1L platinum + pembrolizumab with SD/PR/CR).
primary_endpoints: Progression‑free survival (PFS) by BICR per RECIST v1.1 in ITT population; Overall survival (OS) in overall population and in CR/PR population
target_population: Adults (≥18 years) with histologically/cytologically confirmed advanced (Stage IIIB/IIIC not amenable to definitive chemoradiotherapy) or metastatic (Stage IV) non‑small cell lung cancer without known targetable driver alteration; must have completed 4–6 cycles of first‑line platinum‑based induction chemotherapy with pembrolizumab and achieved stable disease, partial response, or complete response; ECOG 0‑1; adequate organ and bone‑marrow function; life expectancy ≥12 weeks; able to swallow oral study drug; tumor specimens must be submitted. Exclusions include mixed small‑cell lung cancer or sarcomatoid variant, prior PARP inhibitor use, symptomatic brain metastases/leptomeningeal disease/CNS hemorrhage, active or prior autoimmune disease, high‑dose chronic steroids, interstitial or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids, history of MDS/AML, active tuberculosis, among others.
trial_progress_evidence: Primary Completion (Actual) 2025-02-26; Study Completion (Estimated) 2026-02-06; Enrollment (Actual) 666; Study record Last Update Posted (Estimated) 2025-09-29; Last Verified 2025-09
previous_phase_results_summary: No previous phase results summary available
secondary_endpoints: Time to progression in the CNS (RANO‑BM); Number of participants with adverse events (AEs), serious adverse events (SAEs) and adverse events of special interest (AESIs); Plasma concentrations of niraparib (PK); PFS and OS by PD‑L1 status; Change from baseline in health‑related quality of life, functioning and symptoms (EORTC QLQ‑C30)
comparator_control_arm: Placebo + pembrolizumab
study_design: Phase 3; interventional; randomized; parallel assignment; triple‑masked (participant, care provider, investigator); placebo‑controlled; multicenter; treatment intent
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or modifications reported
mechanism_of_action: Niraparib is an oral PARP‑1/2 inhibitor that impairs single‑strand DNA break repair, leading to synthetic lethality in tumor cells with DNA‑repair deficiencies. Pembrolizumab is an anti‑PD‑1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the PD‑1 receptor, enhancing anti‑tumor T‑cell activity.

title: Sacituzumab tirumotecan (sac‑TMT; SKB264/MK‑2870) + Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) Phase 3 Trial (NCT06448312)
description: Phase 3 randomized open‑label trial (NCT06448312) of sacituzumab tirumotecan (ADC) plus pembrolizumab in PD‑L1‑positive advanced NSCLC (first‑line). Primary endpoint PFS met; OS trend positive. Sponsored by Sichuan Kelun‑Biotech, conducted in China, with regulatory plans for supplemental NDA in China.
oncology_drug_check: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer; Drug/Biologic (Sacituzumab tirumotecan, Pembrolizumab)
development_status: The phase 3 trial met its primary endpoint (PFS), and overall survival showed a positive trend. The sponsor (Sichuan Kelun-Biotech) is pursuing regulatory steps in China, with plans for supplemental NDA/marketing authorization. The program is in active advanced clinical/regulatory development with likely regulatory submission planning in China.
drug_indication: The investigational combination of sacituzumab tirumotecan (SKB264/sac-TMT) + pembrolizumab (Keytruda) is being evaluated as a first-line treatment for PD-L1-positive (TPS ≥1%) locally advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).
mechanism_context: Sacituzumab tirumotecan (sac-TMT; SKB264/MK-2870) is an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) directed at TROP-2. It is combined with pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1) to enhance antitumor activity by delivering a cytotoxic payload to TROP-2-expressing tumor cells while engaging immune checkpoint blockade.
progress_timeline_date: The study was active during the 2023-2025 timeframe. The ClinicalTrials.gov record for NCT06448312 was updated in 2025. The sponsor publicly announced trial results in late 2025, with a press release dated November 24, 2025.
trial_design: This is a Phase 3, randomized, open-label, parallel-assignment study with 1:1 randomization, primarily for treatment. The experimental arm receives SKB264 (sac-TMT) on Days 1, 15, and 29 of each 6-week cycle plus pembrolizumab on Day 1 of each 6-week cycle. The comparator arm receives pembrolizumab monotherapy on Day 1 of each 6-week cycle. Key eligibility criteria include adults aged 18-75 years with histologically/cytologically confirmed stage IIIB/IIIC or IV NSCLC not amenable to radical therapy, PD-L1 tumor proportion score (TPS) ≥1%, ECOG 0-1, measurable disease per RECIST 1.1, and no prior systemic therapy in the locally advanced/metastatic setting. The primary endpoint is progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints typically including overall survival (OS), objective response rate (ORR), duration of response (DoR), and safety.
interim_analysis_results: At the interim analysis, the combination demonstrated statistically significant PFS improvement and a positive OS trend versus pembrolizumab monotherapy.
biomarker_requirements: PD‑L1 TPS ≥1%
key_competing_drugs_trials: KEYNOTE‑189, KEYNOTE‑407, IMpower150 (ABCP)
current_standard_of_care: Pembrolizumab monotherapy for high PD‑L1, pembrolizumab + platinum‑based chemotherapy, and the atezolizumab + bevacizumab + carboplatin + paclitaxel (ABCP) regimen are current first‑line standards for metastatic NSCLC.
primary_endpoints: Progression‑Free Survival (PFS) assessed by Blinded Independent Central Review (BICR)
target_population: Adults 18‑75 with histologically confirmed locally advanced (Stage IIIB/IIIC) or metastatic (Stage IV) NSCLC, PD‑L1 TPS ≥1%, no prior systemic therapy, ECOG 0‑1, adequate organ function; excludes active second malignancy, uncontrolled cardiac disease, prior checkpoint inhibitor, TROP2‑targeted therapy, recent major surgery, pregnancy, etc.
trial_progress_evidence: Recruiting status confirmed; interim analysis showed statistically significant PFS benefit and positive OS trend.
previous_phase_results_summary: Phase 1/2 first‑in‑human study (NCT04152499) of sacituzumab tirumotecan monotherapy in advanced solid tumors showed preclinical antitumor activity and reported early clinical results, establishing rationale for the current phase 3 trial.
secondary_endpoints: Overall Survival (OS), Objective Response Rate (ORR), Disease Control Rate (DCR), Duration of Response (DoR), Time to Response (TTR), Investigator‑assessed PFS
comparator_control_arm: Pembrolizumab monotherapy (active comparator)
study_design: Phase 3, randomized, open‑label, parallel assignment
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or protocol modifications reported at the time of interim topline announcements
mechanism_of_action: A TROP2‑directed antibody‑drug conjugate delivering a belotecan‑derived topoisomerase I inhibitor payload through a stable sulfonyl pyrimidine‑CL2A carbonate linker, leading to targeted DNA damage in tumor cells.

title: Imdelltra
description: Imdelltra is a DLL3‑targeting T‑cell engager evaluated in a Phase 3 trial (NCT06117774) for maintenance therapy after first‑line treatment of limited‑stage small‑cell lung cancer (LS‑SCLC). The study compares Imdelltra monotherapy versus placebo, with progression‑free survival as the primary endpoint. Imfinzi is an approved comparator in this setting.
oncology_drug_check: small cell lung cancer (SCLC) – drug
development_status: The DeLLphi-306 trial is currently recruiting participants. However, the overall development status of tarlatamab (Imdelltra) is advanced, as Amgen publicly reported positive top-line overall survival data for the drug in April 2025, indicating successful clinical outcomes.
drug_indication: The drug being studied is tarlatamab (Imdelltra). It is being evaluated as maintenance therapy for patients with limited-stage small-cell lung cancer (LS-SCLC) who have completed concurrent chemoradiation and have not experienced disease progression.
mechanism_context: Tarlatamab (marketed as Imdelltra) is a DLL3-targeted bispecific T-cell engager. It functions by binding to DLL3 on tumor cells and CD3 on T cells, thereby redirecting T cells to mediate the lysis of tumor cells. DLL3 is specifically expressed on small-cell lung cancer (SCLC) cells, making it a relevant therapeutic target in this cancer type.
progress_timeline_date: The DeLLphi-306 trial started on 2024-02-20. The primary completion date and study completion date are both listed as 2030-03-31. A key milestone occurred in April 2025, when Amgen publicly reported that Imdelltra demonstrated superior overall survival in small-cell lung cancer, suggesting an interim analysis or top-line results were announced.
trial_design: DeLLphi-306 is a Phase 3, randomized (1:1), double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter, parallel-group study. It enrolls approximately 400 adult participants (aged ≥18) with histologically/cytologically confirmed limited-stage small-cell lung cancer (LS-SCLC) who have completed concurrent chemoradiation without progression (CR/PR/SD) and have an ECOG Performance Status of 0 or 1. Patients with extensive-stage SCLC, interstitial lung disease, prior DLL3-pathway inhibitors, or recent major surgery are typically excluded. The experimental arm receives intravenous tarlatamab (AMG 757) on Cycle 1 Day 1, 8, and 15, then every 2 weeks (Q2W) thereafter, with each cycle lasting 28 days. The comparator arm receives placebo intravenously on the same schedule. The primary endpoint is progression-free survival (PFS) assessed by blinded independent central review (BICR) per RECIST 1.1. Key secondary endpoints include overall survival (OS), investigator-assessed PFS, objective response rate, disease control rate, duration of response, time to progression, pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, and safety.
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
geographic_scope: Argentina, Australia, Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Bulgaria, China, Colombia, France, Germany, Greece, Hong Kong, Italy, Japan, Mexico, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Singapore, South Korea, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, Taiwan, Turkey (Türkiye), United Kingdom, United States, Israel
key_competing_drugs_trials: Durvalumab consolidation (ADRIATIC)
current_standard_of_care: Durvalumab (Imfinzi) consolidation/maintenance after concurrent chemoradiotherapy is FDA‑approved for limited‑stage SCLC and is considered practice‑changing with overall survival and progression‑free survival benefits.
primary_endpoints: Progression‑free survival (PFS) by blinded independent central review (RECIST 1.1); Overall survival (OS)
target_population: Adults (≥18) with histologically/cytologically confirmed limited-stage small-cell lung cancer who have completed concurrent chemoradiation without progression (CR/PR/SD), ECOG performance status 0‑1, and adequate organ function. Excludes extensive-stage disease, prior DLL3 inhibitor exposure, interstitial lung disease, active infections, and concurrent systemic anti‑cancer therapy.
trial_progress_evidence: Recruiting status with many global sites; study start 2024-02-20; primary and study completion estimated for 2030-03-31; first posted 2023-11-07, last updated 2025-10-22; numerous sites listed as Recruiting, indicating active conduct.
previous_phase_results_summary: Phase‑1 data showed a 40% objective response rate and median overall survival of 14.3 months in advanced SCLC, with cytokine release syndrome occurring in ~56% of patients (mostly low‑grade and transient). Subsequent phase‑3 DeLLphi‑1304 in relapsed SCLC demonstrated overall‑survival superiority versus chemotherapy, leading to FDA approval in extensive‑stage disease.
