secondary_endpoints: PFS by investigator assessment; Objective response rate (ORR); Disease control rate (DCR); Duration of response (DOR); PFS at 6 months, 1 year, 2 years; OS at 6 months, 1 year, 2 years, 3 years; Time to progression (TTP); Treatment‑emergent adverse events (TEAEs); Serum concentration of tarlatamab (PK); Anti‑tarlatamab antibody incidence (immunogenicity)
comparator_control_arm: Placebo
study_design: Phase 3; interventional; randomized; parallel assignment; double-blind (participant, investigator); placebo-controlled; treatment
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or protocol modifications reported on the registry record.
mechanism_of_action: Tarlatamab‑dlle is a bispecific T‑cell engager that binds DLL3 on tumor cells and CD3 on T cells, triggering T‑cell activation, cytokine release, and lysis of DLL3‑expressing cells.

title: LYNPARZA™ (MK-7339)
description: LYNPARZA™ (olaparib, MK‑7339) is a poly (ADP‑ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibitor being tested in a Phase 3 randomized, double‑blind, placebo‑controlled trial (NCT04380636) for patients with advanced non‑small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The study enrolls adult participants who have progressed after prior platinum‑based chemotherapy, requires measurable disease, and mandates tumor testing for homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) or specific BRCA1/2 mutations. The primary endpoint is overall survival; secondary endpoints include progression‑free survival, objective response rate, and safety. The trial is sponsored by Merck in collaboration with AstraZeneca and holds Fast Track and Orphan Drug designations in the United States.
oncology_drug_check: Non‑Small Cell Lung Cancer; drug (olaparib) and biologic (pembrolizumab)
development_status: The drug is in active Phase 3 development under the KEYLYNK-012 program, sponsored by Merck (MSD) in collaboration with AstraZeneca. The trial is currently active and ongoing, with an estimated completion in 2026-2027. The user noted Fast Track and Orphan Drug designations in the U.S., which would be recorded in sponsor/regulatory filings and press releases.
drug_indication: The drug under study is LYNPARZA™ (olaparib; developed as MK-7339 in collaboration between MSD/Merck and AstraZeneca). The specific indication being studied is unresectable, locally advanced (stage III) non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) in patients receiving definitive concurrent chemoradiation (CCRT). The trial evaluates the incorporation of pembrolizumab with CCRT followed by maintenance pembrolizumab with or without olaparib, compared to CCRT followed by durvalumab.
mechanism_context: LYNPARZA (olaparib, MK-7339) is a PARP inhibitor. PARP inhibitors block poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase-mediated repair of single-strand DNA breaks, leading to accumulation of DNA damage and tumor cell death, particularly in tumors with homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) such as BRCA1/2-mutant cancers. The KEYLYNK-012 program investigates whether adding olaparib to anti-PD-1 therapy after concurrent chemoradiation (CCRT) can improve outcomes in unresectable, locally advanced stage III NSCLC by leveraging DNA damage and immunomodulatory effects of PARP inhibition. Olaparib is approved for use in advanced ovarian, breast, pancreatic, and prostate cancer.
progress_timeline_date: The study started on 2020-07-06. The estimated primary completion date is 2026-07-06, and the estimated study completion date is 2027-02-15. The estimated enrollment is 870 participants.
trial_design: This is a Phase 3, interventional, multicenter, randomized, placebo- and active-controlled, double-blind study (blinded between olaparib vs placebo within pembrolizumab arms). Participants are randomized 1:1:1 into three groups: Group A receives pembrolizumab + CCRT followed by pembrolizumab + placebo; Group B receives pembrolizumab + CCRT followed by pembrolizumab + olaparib; and Group C receives CCRT followed by durvalumab (active comparator). Key eligibility criteria include unresectable, locally advanced stage III NSCLC planned for definitive CCRT. The trial's rationale involves DNA damage/HRD biology, with biomarker testing for PARP sensitivity. Primary and secondary endpoints listed on ClinicalTrials.gov include incidence of adverse events, discontinuation rate due to AE, objective response rate (ORR) per RECIST 1.1 by BICR, and change from baseline in role functioning using EORTC QLQ-C30.
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
geographic_scope: United States, Turkey, Romania, Poland, Norway, Latvia, Lithuania, Italy, Hungary, United Kingdom, France, Estonia, Spain, Germany, Czech Republic, Canada, Argentina
key_competing_drugs_trials: Durvalumab (PACIFIC)
current_standard_of_care: Consolidation durvalumab after chemoradiotherapy (the PACIFIC regimen) is the standard of care for unresectable stage III NSCLC.
primary_endpoints: PFS per RECIST v1.1 by blinded independent central review, Overall Survival
target_population: Adults (≥18) with histologically or cytologically confirmed, previously untreated, unresectable stage IIIA‑IIIC non‑small cell lung cancer, not eligible for curative surgery; both squamous and non‑squamous histologies are allowed (pemetrexed excluded for squamous).
sponsor_developer: Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC (Merck & Co., Inc.)
trial_progress_evidence: Status Active, not recruiting; Study Start Actual 2020-07-06; Primary Completion Estimated 2026-07-06; Study Completion Estimated 2027-02-15.
line_of_therapy: Definitive CCRT setting for unresectable stage III; maintenance/consolidation phase after chemoradiation.
previous_phase_results_summary: Earlier studies demonstrated pembrolizumab plus concurrent chemoradiotherapy activity in stage III NSCLC (e.g., KEYNOTE‑799) and acceptable safety of olaparib combined with pembrolizumab in advanced solid tumors, providing the rationale for the phase 3 evaluation.
secondary_endpoints: Safety/tolerability, objective response rate, duration of response, patient-reported outcomes, exploratory PFS on next line of therapy
comparator_control_arm: CCRT followed by durvalumab; also placebo vs olaparib within the pembrolizumab maintenance arms.
study_design: Phase 3, multicenter, randomized (1:1:1), double-blind (olaparib vs placebo), placebo‑ and active‑controlled; induction with CCRT + pembrolizumab followed by maintenance pembrolizumab ± olaparib or CCRT → durvalumab.
combination_partners: Pembrolizumab; concurrent chemoradiation with platinum‑based chemotherapy (carboplatin, cisplatin, etoposide, paclitaxel, pemetrexed as appropriate) and thoracic radiotherapy
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or modifications reported
mechanism_of_action: Olaparib is a potent PARP inhibitor that blocks PARP‑mediated DNA repair, leading to cancer cell death, especially in tumors with homologous recombination deficiency.

title: intismeran autogene (V940)
description: intismeran autogene (V940) is an mRNA‑based individualized neoantigen therapy for melanoma. It is being evaluated in combination with KEYTRUDA. The trial (NCT05933577) targets melanoma patients; the exact phase, enrollment numbers, and endpoints are not specified in the provided sources.
oncology_drug_check: High‑risk melanoma (C43.9); individualized neoantigen therapy (mRNA‑based) / drug (pembrolizumab)
development_status: The trial (INTerpath-001) is an active, ongoing Phase 3 study, as registered on clinicaltrials.gov. The sponsor is Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC, with ModernaTX, Inc. as a collaborator. The initiation of this Phase 3 study follows earlier clinical results evaluating mRNA-4157/V940 plus pembrolizumab, which showed evidence of improved recurrence-free survival and distant-metastasis-free survival. Corporate press materials have reported multi-year (3-year) data indicating sustained improvement in recurrence-free survival and distant-metastasis-free survival for the combination compared to pembrolizumab alone, supporting its continued development into this Phase 3 adjuvant program.
drug_indication: The drug is Intismeran autogene (V940), also known as mRNA-4157, an individualized neoantigen therapy. It is being studied for the adjuvant treatment of participants with high-risk, resected Stage II-IV melanoma to prevent recurrence after surgical resection. The trial compares V940 plus pembrolizumab versus placebo plus pembrolizumab.
mechanism_context: Intismeran autogene (V940; formerly mRNA-4157) is an individualized neoantigen therapy, an mRNA-based vaccine tailored to a patient’s tumor mutanome and HLA type. It encodes up to approximately 34 patient-specific tumor neoantigens in an mRNA construct delivered in a lipid nanoparticle formulation. The mRNA is translated in host cells, and the encoded neoantigen peptides enter endogenous antigen-processing and presentation pathways, provoking de novo and/or boosted tumor-specific CD8+ and CD4+ T-cell responses. The therapy is administered with pembrolizumab (an anti-PD-1 agent) because the vaccine increases neoantigen-specific T-cell responses while PD-1 blockade reduces inhibitory signaling, thereby enhancing antitumor immunity.
progress_timeline_date: The study started on 2023-07-19. The estimated primary completion date is 2029-10-26, and the estimated study completion date is 2030-09-26. The estimated enrollment for the trial is 1,089 participants.
trial_design: The trial, NCT05933577 (INTerpath-001), is a Phase 3, randomized, double-blind, placebo- and active-comparator-controlled study. It involves two arms: an experimental arm receiving Intismeran autogene (IM) plus Pembrolizumab (IV), and an active comparator arm receiving Placebo plus Pembrolizumab. Intismeran autogene is administered via intramuscular injection for up to 9 doses every 3 weeks, while pembrolizumab is given intravenously for up to 9 doses every 6 weeks. Treatment continues until recurrence, unacceptable toxicity, or for a total duration of up to approximately 56 weeks. The study uses a parallel assignment model with triple masking (Participant, Investigator, Outcomes Assessor), described as double-blind. The primary endpoint is Distant Metastasis-Free Survival (DMFS), defined as the time from study start to cancer spread or death from any cause, with a timeframe of up to approximately 85 months. Key secondary endpoints include Overall Survival (OS) (up to approximately 85 months), safety endpoints (number of participants with adverse events and discontinuations due to AEs over approximately 56-68 weeks), and quality-of-life change assessed by the EORTC QLQ-C30 global health/QoL score. The patient population includes adults (18 years and older) of all sexes with high-risk Stage II-IV melanoma in the adjuvant setting. Exclusions include other known cancers that have metastasized or required treatment within the past 3 years, severe reactions to study medications, or unrecovered major surgery/ongoing surgical complications. The estimated enrollment is 1,089 participants.
interim_analysis_results: A planned 3‑year interim analysis (presented at ASCO 2024) showed sustained RFS benefit (HR 0.51) and DMFS benefit (HR 0.38) for V940 + pembrolizumab versus pembrolizumab alone.
related_approved_in_trial_drugs: Pembrolizumab, nivolumab, dabrafenib + trametinib, ipilimumab, relatlimab + nivolumab, other BRAF/MEK inhibitor combinations
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker requirements
key_competing_drugs_trials: NEO‑PV‑01 (personalized neoantigen vaccine) + PD‑1 blockade, other individualized neoantigen vaccine platforms (e.g., iNeST), standard PD‑1–based regimens such as pembrolizumab or nivolumab alone
current_standard_of_care: In the adjuvant setting for resected high‑risk melanoma, anti‑PD‑1 monotherapy (pembrolizumab or nivolumab) is standard; for BRAF‑mutant disease, dabrafenib + trametinib is an option. In the metastatic setting, anti‑PD‑1 regimens and BRAF/MEK inhibitors are standard.
primary_endpoints: Recurrence‑free survival (RFS)
target_population: Adults with completely resected, high‑risk stage II‑IV melanoma (post‑surgery) who are candidates for adjuvant therapy.
sponsor_developer: Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC (Merck/MSD) in collaboration with ModernaTX, Inc.
trial_progress_evidence: Trial status is closed to accrual; estimated primary completion date is 2029‑10‑26 and study completion is 2030‑09‑26.
previous_phase_results_summary: In the phase 2b KEYNOTE‑942 study (mRNA‑4157 + pembrolizumab, N=157) for resected high‑risk melanoma, the combination improved recurrence‑free survival (HR 0.51) and distant metastasis‑free survival (HR 0.38) versus pembrolizumab alone, with a safety profile comparable to pembrolizumab alone.
secondary_endpoints: Distant metastasis‑free survival, overall survival, safety/tolerability (adverse events), quality of life (EORTC QLQ‑C30)
comparator_control_arm: Placebo plus pembrolizumab
study_design: Phase 3, randomized, double‑blind, placebo‑ and active‑comparator‑controlled
safety_signals_modifications: No safety signals or protocol modifications have been reported publicly for INTerpath‑001; safety appears comparable to pembrolizumab alone with minimal additional adverse events.
mechanism_of_action: Intismeran autogene (V940) is an individualized mRNA‑based neoantigen vaccine that encodes up to 34 patient‑specific neoantigens in a lipid‑nanoparticle formulation. After intramuscular injection the mRNA is translated in vivo, the neoantigen peptides are presented on HLA molecules, and they stimulate de‑novo and existing T‑cell responses against tumor‑specific antigens, enhancing the activity of checkpoint inhibition.
